items.title: SA police say man dies while resisting arrest in Adelaide CBD

children.content: A man has died in the Adelaide CBD after police say he became unresponsive during an attempted arrest this afternoon.

gallery.altText: Police blocking area near a pub and hotel

thumbnail.altText: Police blocking area near a pub and hotel

items.title: Insect repellent release leaves 11 staff sick at major SA hospital

children.content: Eleven staff at an Adelaide hospital have become unwell and a dozen emergency beds have been closed, after the accidental release of an "outdoor insect repellent" inside the facility.

image.altText: A fire truck at the Flinders Medical Centre.

items.title: Pies make statement with impressive win over listless Crows

children.content: Collingwood franks its strong run of form with an impressive 34-point win over Adelaide, as Crows fans defy pleas from the club and boo Isaac Quaynor.

image.altText: Ed Allan celebrates a goal with teammates

items.title: Former MP convicted of three extra counts of deception

children.content: Former SA state MP Fraser Ellis now stands convicted of seven counts of deception after failing to have his original convictions thrown out on appeal, and having several acquittals overturned.

image.altText: Fraser Ellis outside court

items.title: Indigenous-owned town hopes giant fish statue will attract tourists

children.content: On the far west coast of SA, this Aboriginal community requires a permit to enter, meaning people usually drive on by. But new public art at the roadhouse, bought by the community, aims to boost tourism and financial independence.

image.altText: A large sculpture of a fish is on stilts in a shed setting

items.title: Family takes racing club to court over 'enormous' hidden rubbish dump

children.content: A family discovered a large waste dump, allegedly created by a South Australian horse racing club, as they began foundation work on their home, court documents allege.

gallery.altText: A woman and man standing together with a rubbish pit behind them.
A photo of a large mound of rubbish.

thumbnail.altText: A woman and man standing together with a rubbish pit behind them.
A photo of a large mound of rubbish.

items.title: Adelaide man charged with producing AI child exploitation material

children.content: SA Police have arrested and charged a man from Adelaide's northern suburbs for the production and possession of AI-generated child exploitation material.

image.altText: An SA police car parked on the side of a street with trees on the opposite side

items.title: AFL's first publicly gay player calls for respect in Crows-Pies match

children.content: Four months after becoming the first current or former AFL player to publicly come out as gay, Leigh Ryswyk is urging fans not to boo the Collingwood player targeted by Crow Izak Rankine’s homophobic slur.

image.altText: Leigh Ryswyk Header image

items.title: Kiana started weightlifting after an injury. Now, she's a Comm Games athlete

children.content: Kiana Elliott thought gymnastics would be her pathway to the podium, but turned to weightlifting after an injury forced her to retire.

gallery.altText: Kiana Elliott at a gym, bending down with her hands on weights as she prepares to lift them. She is dressed in a blue t-shirt

thumbnail.altText: Kiana Elliott at a gym, bending down with her hands on weights as she prepares to lift them. She is dressed in a blue t-shirt

items.title: 'Nervous' investors retreat from housing market as downturn broadens

children.content: Australia's property market has entered a downturn after both house and unit prices fell for the first time in more than three years, bringing an end to the recent housing boom.

image.altText: a skyline with houses

items.title: Unlawful excavation of river cliff disturbs Aboriginal ancestral remains

children.content: A cliff site in South Australia's Riverland, home to Aboriginal ancestral remains, has been excavated without authorisation.

image.altText: An Aboriginal woman in front of a damaged cliff face.

items.title: SA government hits 'pause' on compulsory acquisition of popular restaurant

children.content: The state government last week announced a $61.1 million preschool and primary school would be built on a 5.5-hectare parcel of land in Mount Barker's south-east, including on the site of popular Thai restaurant Wok The Duck.

image.altText: A man stands in front of a artistically graffitied wall with his arms crossed.

items.title: Rain cancels outback reunions and events for second year

children.content: Flood-damaged roads and soggy racetracks mean some of the outback's favourite events are being cancelled and keeping tourists away.

image.altText: A young girl riding a horse, with a woman leading the horse

items.title: Antisemitism 'using Zionism as an excuse', rabbi tells royal commission

children.content: A recent wave of antisemitism has been far-reaching, even targeting Jewish people who are critical of the Israeli government, a royal commission probing the phenomenon has heard.

image.altText: A woman standing.

items.title: Why highly advanced rogue AI has experts scared

children.content: An experimental AI broke out of its containment and hacked a company. Why has this got experts worried?

image.altText: OpenAI logo is seen in this illustration taken June 11, 2026.

items.title: Researcher shocked as orca blows up a sunfish in front of her

children.content: Underwater video captures the moment killer whales in the Gulf of California slam into a sunfish, causing it to explode.
Researchers say the orcas may use the technique to make it easier for juvenile killer whales to feed or they do it as a fun group bonding exercise.

gallery.altText: Two black and white whale sharks in a cloud of white fleshy scraps against a blue backdrop.

thumbnail.altText: Two black and white whale sharks in a cloud of white fleshy scraps against a blue backdrop.

avatar.alt: A man in a blue checked shirt and blue jacket with a beard and long hair.

items.title: Penrith flyer breaks record in comeback Panthers win

children.content: Thomas Jenkins has claimed a slice of history in Penrith's unconvincing win over Parramatta in the western Sydney derby.

image.altText: A man scores a try in a rugby league match

items.title: Taiwanese woman sentenced for attempted murder of Australian exchange student

children.content: Taiwanese media has reported Yang Jinping has been sentenced for the attempted murder of Brisbane exchange student Alex Shorey in 2023.

image.altText: A young man smiles at the camera

items.title: Children in state care being lured into 'trap houses'

children.content: The ABC has spoken with experts, advocates and residential care workers as part of a 19-month investigation into the sexual exploitation of children in state care.

gallery.altText: A close up of a young woman from behind who looks out at the Brisbane River wearing a black hoodie and with her hair in a bun.
A close up of a woman's hands which are clasped together.
A woman stands on the edge of the Brisbane River leaning on a railing with a tree in focus in the foreground.

avatar.alt: A portrait shot of Eden standing next to a tree.

items.title: Right-wing conference arrives in UK to snoring, empty seats and bad reviews

children.content: In 10 years at the ABC, I've had some strange experiences. I can now add last week's CPAC conference in London to the list, and it's going somewhere near the top, says Riley Stuart in London.

image.altText: A wide shot of a half full auditorium with a woman in blue dress on stage in distance

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: Judge rejects Woodside's attempt to uncover stink-bomb protest organisers

children.content: Energy giant Woodside fails in its Supreme Court bid to force climate activists to hand over documents about the planning of a stink-bomb protest which prompted the evacuation of its Perth headquarters.

image.altText: woodside

items.title: Reading has many benefits, here's how to fit it in to a busy life

children.content: More than half of us want to read more. Here's how to find your gateway genre and make that happen.

gallery.altText: Overhead shot of woman holding a book and a phone with an Instagram feed in either hand.

thumbnail.altText: Overhead shot of woman holding a book and a phone with an Instagram feed in either hand.

items.title: The intriguing film that casts the 'superior' Jude Law as the insidious Putin

children.content: Cannes darling Oliver Assayas tackles the shadowy forces of our time in his latest film, The Wizard of the Kremlin.

image.altText: A man in a suit talks on the phone

items.title: Nintendo raised prices due to US tariffs. Now, it doesn't want to pass on refunds

children.content: Nintendo has argued it has no duty to pass its refunds onto US consumers. It's the latest in a lengthy tariff fight.

image.altText: A handheld gaming device.

items.title: Outback hubs prepare for 'amazingly majestic' 2028 solar eclipse

children.content: About 10,000 eclipse chasers are expected to descend on the Northern Territory's Barkly region in two years to stand in the shadow of the moon.

gallery.altText: A man wearing special glasses over his eyes.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing special glasses over his eyes.

items.title: Japanese PM's sleep revelation sparks concern

children.content: It's unlikely Sanae Takaichi intended to start a national conversation when she tweeted about how much rest she gets. A sleep expert says her schedule would be exacting a heavy toll.

image.altText: Woman in light blue blazer

items.title: Melbourne football team to play without audience after sexist fan conduct

children.content: The Preston Lions, associated with the Macedonian Australian community, will be forced to play without spectators after some of its fans displayed offensive banners at a game last weekend.

image.altText: A man in red soccer attire runs towward a ball during a match.

items.title: Countdown clock unveiled for Brisbane 2032 Olympics

children.content: The Brisbane City Council has installed a countdown clock to mark the milestone, while an iconic Kangaroo mascot has returned in Moreton Bay.

image.altText: A woman and a man pose with their hands up in front of a digital clock.

items.title: Sovereign citizen question to gun owners shut down by senior officer

children.content: A senior police officer slams the strategy of writing to some Tasmanian gun owners asking them to confirm or deny whether they aligned with sovereign citizen ideology.

image.altText: Tasmania Police officers on patrol wearing luminescent vests, generic image.

items.title: Record $60k fine for man behind 'brazen' dump site that will cost $1m to clean

children.content: The owner of one of Victoria's worst illegal dump sites has been handed a record fine of $60,000 and ordered to pay an expected $1 million to clean up the polluted property in Melbourne's outer north-east.

gallery.altText: A large pile of rubbish at a dump with smoke on a regional property

thumbnail.altText: A large pile of rubbish at a dump with smoke on a regional property

items.title: Sydney man fined for wearing Nazi symbol says he's 'trashed' his life

children.content: A man who wore a T-shirt with a banned Nazi symbol outside the antisemitism royal commission in Sydney has been convicted and fined $1,500.

image.altText: Ian Minus leaves court after being fined for wearing a swastika T-shirt.

items.title: Unemployment rate steady for now, but could increase in coming months

children.content: The unemployment rate was steady in June at 4.4 per cent but underemployment has risen.

image.altText: Shoppers on Pitt Street mall on Boxing Day

items.title: Tongan women 'auctioned off' for marriage in China

children.content: The ABC has spoken to multiple Tongan women who have been lured into an arranged marriage syndicate operating in China, with allegations of passports being confiscated and tens of thousands of dollars changing hands.

image.altText: Close-up of a woman wearing a face mask.

items.title: Mamdani 'criminal' video sparks debate about Netanyahu arrest warrant

children.content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani concedes he cannot order the arrest of Benjamin Netanyahu and calls on the US government to take action, but a former international prosecutor warns it could be complicated.

image.altText: Mamdani sits with his hands clasped at a wooden desk.

items.title: Sydney man's home gets so hot he has to replace his TV

children.content: A new study led by Western Sydney University researchers has found a lack of thermal safety standards in social housing is leading to tenants being exposed to unsafe extreme heat events.

gallery.altText: a man wearing a blue t-shirt standing at his front door

thumbnail.altText: a man wearing a blue t-shirt standing at his front door
